note: VariationCache is a redirect-aware cache backend wrapper that adds cache-context support; it is a backport that was merged into Drupal core 10.2 and now ships mainly as a class_alias compatibility shim. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: In Drupal I need a cache that varies by cache context (like user.permissions) without building context-specific cache IDs myself. Which service does VariationCache expose and how do I get a bin wrapper from it?
evals.expected_output: Use the variation_cache_factory service; call ->get($bin) on it to get a VariationCacheInterface object that wraps the named cache bin and adds cache-context awareness.

evals.prompt: What are the arguments to VariationCacheInterface::set() and what is the difference between the two cacheability arguments?
evals.expected_output: set(array $keys, $data, CacheableDependencyInterface $cacheability, CacheableDependencyInterface $initial_cacheability). $cacheability is the full/folded cache metadata after bubbling; $initial_cacheability is the pre-bubbling metadata known before other systems adjusted it. Keys is an array of cache keys, not a single cid.

evals.prompt: Under what condition does VariationCache::set() throw a LogicException, and when does it silently store nothing?
evals.expected_output: It throws \LogicException when the initial (pre-bubbling) cache contexts are not fully contained in the full cacheability's contexts (the full set must be a superset). It silently stores nothing when the cacheability's max-age is 0 (uncacheable).

evals.prompt: If I call delete() or invalidate() on a VariationCache item, does it remove every context variation of that item or just one?
evals.expected_output: Only the active variation for the current request is affected, to stay consistent with get(); other context variations of the same keys are not deleted or invalidated.

evals.prompt: Is the VariationCache contrib module still needed on Drupal 11 / 10.2, and what does the module actually do on those core versions?
evals.expected_output: It is deprecated because the code was merged into Drupal core 10.2+. On those versions the module only registers class_alias() entries mapping \Drupal\variationcache\Cache\* to the core \Drupal\Core\Cache\* classes; you can uninstall it and reference the core classes directly.

evals.prompt: I want to decorate VariationCacheFactory. Which interface do I implement, what single method must it provide, and what services does the core factory take?
evals.expected_output: Implement VariationCacheFactoryInterface, which defines get($bin) returning a VariationCacheInterface. The bundled factory is constructed from request_stack, cache_factory, and cache_contexts_manager; decorate the variation_cache_factory service and wrap those calls.

evals.prompt: Does VariationCache add any admin UI, config form, permissions, or Drush commands I need to configure?
evals.expected_output: No. VariationCache has no UI, no config, no permissions, no routes, and no Drush commands. It is purely a developer/API dependency providing the variation_cache_factory service and related classes.
